risks.title: Unauthorized User Access
risks.description: Users may gain access to systems or data without proper authorization due to weak provisioning, approval, or access review processes.
risks.mitigation: Require documented access approval, role-based access controls, MFA, and periodic user access reviews.

risks.title: Privilege Escalation
risks.description: Users may obtain elevated privileges beyond business need, increasing the impact of account compromise or misuse.
risks.mitigation: Enforce least privilege, privileged access approval, privileged access reviews, and segregation of duties.

risks.title: Missing Multi-Factor Authentication
risks.description: Accounts may be compromised if multi-factor authentication is not enforced for privileged, remote, or user access.
risks.mitigation: Enable MFA for all users where possible and require MFA for privileged and remote access.

risks.title: Dormant User Accounts
risks.description: Inactive accounts may remain enabled and become targets for unauthorized access.
risks.mitigation: Identify inactive accounts, review access periodically, and disable or remove accounts that are no longer needed.

risks.title: Shared Account Misuse
risks.description: Shared accounts may prevent accountability and make unauthorized activity difficult to trace.
risks.mitigation: Prohibit shared accounts where possible, require named user access, and monitor unavoidable shared accounts.

risks.title: Cloud Misconfiguration
risks.description: Cloud resources may be configured insecurely, exposing systems or data to unauthorized access.
risks.mitigation: Use secure baseline configurations, configuration monitoring, periodic reviews, and automated remediation where possible.

risks.title: Unpatched Systems
risks.description: Systems with missing security patches may be exploited through known vulnerabilities.
risks.mitigation: Maintain patch management, vulnerability scanning, remediation SLAs, and patch verification.

risks.title: Firewall Rule Misconfiguration
risks.description: Overly permissive firewall rules may expose internal systems or sensitive services to unauthorized networks.
risks.mitigation: Review firewall rules periodically, restrict inbound access, and document approvals for exceptions.

risks.title: Endpoint Compromise
risks.description: Employee endpoints may be compromised through malware, phishing, or insecure configuration.
risks.mitigation: Deploy endpoint protection, enforce device security baselines, monitor alerts, and keep systems patched.

risks.title: Inadequate Asset Inventory
risks.description: The organization may be unable to protect assets it has not identified or classified.
risks.mitigation: Maintain an asset inventory covering systems, endpoints, applications, cloud resources, and data stores.

risks.title: Unauthorized Production Change
risks.description: Unapproved or untested changes may be deployed to production and compromise security, availability, or integrity.
risks.mitigation: Require change tickets, approvals, testing evidence, deployment records, and emergency change review.

risks.title: Insecure Software Development
risks.description: Applications may contain security weaknesses if secure development practices are not followed.
risks.mitigation: Adopt secure development standards, code review, dependency scanning, security testing, and release controls.

risks.title: Dependency Vulnerability
risks.description: Third-party software dependencies may introduce known vulnerabilities into applications.
risks.mitigation: Use dependency scanning, vulnerability remediation SLAs, and controlled release processes.

risks.title: Missing Security Monitoring
risks.description: Security events may go undetected if systems are not logged, monitored, and reviewed.
risks.mitigation: Centralize logs, define alert rules, assign review responsibilities, and retain monitoring evidence.

risks.title: Log Tampering
risks.description: Logs may be modified or deleted, reducing the organization's ability to investigate incidents.
risks.mitigation: Restrict log access, centralize logs, retain logs securely, and monitor administrative actions.

risks.title: Unreviewed Security Alerts
risks.description: Alerts may not result in timely investigation or response if review ownership and escalation are unclear.
risks.mitigation: Assign alert review owners, define severity criteria, and track investigation and closure.

risks.title: Incident Response Failure
risks.description: Security incidents may not be contained or remediated effectively if response procedures are incomplete or untested.
risks.mitigation: Maintain an incident response plan, train responders, conduct tabletop exercises, and document incidents.

risks.title: Delayed Incident Escalation
risks.description: Incidents may not be escalated to appropriate stakeholders in time to reduce impact.
risks.mitigation: Define escalation criteria, communication paths, response roles, and notification timelines.

risks.title: Incomplete Post-Incident Review
risks.description: Incidents may recur if root causes, lessons learned, and corrective actions are not documented and tracked.
risks.mitigation: Perform post-incident reviews and track corrective actions through completion.

risks.title: Third-Party Security Failure
risks.description: A vendor or service provider may fail to protect systems or data relied on by the organization.
risks.mitigation: Perform vendor due diligence, obtain security reports, define contract requirements, and review vendors periodically.

risks.title: Vendor Access Misuse
risks.description: Vendors with access to systems or data may misuse access or retain access longer than needed.
risks.mitigation: Limit vendor access, require approvals, review access periodically, and remove access when no longer required.

risks.title: Missing Vendor Review
risks.description: Vendors may continue operating without periodic assessment of security, compliance, or service risk.
risks.mitigation: Maintain periodic vendor reviews based on vendor criticality and data access.

risks.title: Backup Failure
risks.description: Backups may be incomplete, unavailable, or unusable during a recovery event.
risks.mitigation: Perform scheduled backups, monitor backup jobs, test restores, and document remediation for failures.

risks.title: Disaster Recovery Failure
risks.description: Critical services may not be restored within expected timeframes during a disruption.
risks.mitigation: Maintain disaster recovery plans, define recovery objectives, and test recovery procedures.

risks.title: Service Availability Failure
risks.description: Systems may become unavailable due to inadequate capacity, monitoring, resilience, or recovery planning.
risks.mitigation: Monitor availability, define capacity thresholds, test continuity procedures, and review incidents.

risks.title: Incomplete Employee Onboarding
risks.description: Employees may begin work without completing required security training, policy acknowledgement, or access approvals.
risks.mitigation: Use onboarding checklists covering training, policy acknowledgement, role assignment, and access approval.

risks.title: Incomplete Employee Offboarding
risks.description: Former employees may retain access to systems, data, or facilities after termination.
risks.mitigation: Trigger access removal from HR termination events and verify deprovisioning across systems.

risks.title: Security Awareness Failure
risks.description: Employees may mishandle systems or data if they do not receive security awareness training.
risks.mitigation: Provide recurring security awareness training and track completion.

risks.title: Insider Threat
risks.description: Employees or contractors may intentionally misuse authorized access to harm systems, data, or operations.
risks.mitigation: Apply least privilege, monitoring, code of conduct, disciplinary processes, and access reviews.

risks.title: Sensitive Data Exposure
risks.description: Sensitive or confidential data may be exposed through improper access, storage, transmission, or disclosure.
risks.mitigation: Classify data, restrict access, encrypt data, monitor transfers, and review data handling practices.

risks.title: Data Leakage
risks.description: Data may leave approved systems or channels without authorization.
risks.mitigation: Restrict exports, enforce approved transfer methods, monitor data movement, and train personnel on data handling.

risks.title: Unauthorized Data Modification
risks.description: Data may be changed without authorization, affecting integrity, reporting, or customer trust.
risks.mitigation: Restrict write access, monitor changes, review privileged activity, and maintain audit logs.

risks.title: Data Retention Failure
risks.description: Data may be retained longer than required or deleted before business, legal, or compliance needs are met.
risks.mitigation: Define retention requirements, implement retention procedures, and review deletion or archival processes.
